reviews.review_msg: Really good lecturer, take home tests, gives you every operating to succeed

He is really funny and engaging. If you need a Quantitative course this is great professor to have for ARCH 285. His lectures, assignments instructions and grading criteria are super clear so it's very easy to understand.

Francesco is very knowledgeable and caring professor. He is charismatic as well, and have a sense of humour. The lectures are material-heavy, but the tests are open-book. So take careful notes. Always ask questions if you have a doubt or need help- and he or his TA will answer promptly. His TA was simply perfect.
He is a great prof and he really cares about his students. His instructions for the tests were very clear and he had good study guides and PowerPoints.
Overall a solid prof, I had him for ARCH 388. Fair marker, and very flexible.

Dr. Berna has been my favourite teacher at SFU. He is very approachable, understanding, and flexible. Study guides are provided for tests, some tests are open book, and the lab assignments are well explained in class. The lectures can have a lot of information, but Dr. Berna throws in some jokes here and there, making it an enjoyable experience.
I am the least science person, but this class was actually decent. As long as you did/do an another archaeology class, then I say you'll do great!

Honestly, I had low expectations, but I enjoyed his class! Sometimes lectures were boring, but he is one of those professors that what he teaches, is exactly the content on quizzes and exams. Hard to find a prof like that at SFU. Overall, I recommend him!!
Nice man, but boring as a bag of rocks. I'm sure Arch 100 is an easy class, but it felt like pulling teeth trying to learn it from him.

Incredibly boring lectures and he is painful to listen to for 2 hours. Turned a 2 hour lab slot into an extra lecture bi weekly, so in 2 weeks there are 6 hours of lecture and 2 hours of lab instead of 4 and 4. Assignments are easy and excel based but I wouldn't take this course based on how he speaks in lecture, it's impossible to stay awake.

super easy class, one midterm 45% & final 55%, all mc + t/f, didnt use the textbook at all, all exam material is from his lectures. he also records lectures & posts them so you dont have to show up. really nice and easy going guy, easy to meet with outside of class + super helpful & i would def recommend this course! awesome gpa booster

This course is perfect for an easy elective. It consumes hardly any time and the material is very simple and easy to understand. Arch 100 is the perfect course to get a good grade in, spend little time on and add some extra credits towards your degree. The course is graded on 3 exams (2 midterms and a final) all MC and cumulative.

If you are looking for a class with minimal assignments and one that you can do with relative ease, this is it. Prof. Berna only grades you on two midterms and a final and each comes with its own exam review guide which lays out EXACTLY what you will be examined on. The content was interesting and he definitely seems passionate about it as well.

Incredibly boring in lectures, luckily everything is posted online. Two midterms (open book, notes everything), 5 assignments (excel based) and a final exam regarding case study. Explanations on the assignments/final exam were confusing and archaeology jargon was used a ton. Overall was worth it for the Q/sci credit, but was not enjoyable.

ARCH100 is graded on 2 midterms and a final, no assignments. Class was pretty well taught, a bit dry a times but still good overall. Exams are easy, but the exam topics given before the exams can be a little bit vague on what exactly to read.

Great lectures. He is very helpful when you ask. Tries his best to make everything fair and easy to understand. Tough grader, you need to work hard, but you can take a lot away from one of his classes.

Francesco was a great professor. He was very helpful, extremely patient, and an easy grader. He gives clear instructions and will get back to you if you ever need help. He made jokes fairly regularly and was very interested in his material. This class was not required for my major, but I'm glad that I took it
his class was very dry and boring. You need to be really good at memorizing, his class was a challenge because of that.

Lots of materials to cover, but overall exams are fair and easy if you know the general idea of everything. He takes attendance at the beginning of class which was the most difficult part for me. But overall, a great prof with a great sense of humor.
amazing prof with a sense of humor, tests are really easy. 1010 would take again

Francesco is an amazing prof! Although he has a strong italian accent he is hilarious. His lectures tend to be quite long since the class has A LOT of material to cover. But overall very good teacher. Always there for questions or concerns and tries his best to help students with make-up quizzes etc... I'm gonna miss him:( !

Berna is an amazing teacher!  His accent is not hard to understand, but rather funny.  He cares about the students and gives them a chance to redeem themselves if they do poorly on a midterm.  The class is based on the textbook and most of the information can be found in there.  I will warn you tough, it is not an easy course there is a lot of info
Test was confusing and questions were tricky
